name: 4 Day Ijen, Papuma Beach, Tumpak Sewu & Bromo Journey from Surabaya
description: 4-day private tour from Surabaya: Ijen Blue Fire crater (midnight hike), Papuma Beach coastal stop, Tumpak Sewu canyon waterfall descent, and Mount Bromo sunrise (private 4WD jeep). Health screening coordinated for Ijen crater access under BBKSDA SE.1658/KSA.9/2024. Fully private — your vehicle, your crew, your group only. Licensed operator NIB 1102230032918.

item.name: Day 1 — Surabaya to Bondowoso for Ijen Staging
item.description: Private transfer from Surabaya to Bondowoso. Check-in and mandatory Ijen health screening conducted the same evening by licensed medical staff (SpO2, blood pressure, heart rate). QR-coded certificate required for BBKSDA crater access gate. Overnight at Riverside Homestay.

item.name: Day 2 — Ijen Crater and Transfer to the Papuma Coastal Side
item.description: Midnight departure to Paltuding base. Guided ascent to Kawah Ijen crater rim (2,386 m). Blue Fire observation in the pre-dawn window. Descent and breakfast, then private transfer south toward the Papuma coastal staging area. Gas masks and trekking poles provided. Overnight at Doho Homestay.

item.name: Day 3 — Papuma Beach and Transfer Toward Tumpak Sewu
item.description: Morning visit to Papuma Beach — white sand, limestone cliffs, and Indian Ocean views on the south Jember coast. Private transfer west toward the Tumpak Sewu staging area. Overnight at Joglo Kecombrang Bromo.

item.name: Day 4 — Tumpak Sewu Canyon, Bromo Sequence, and Return to Surabaya
item.description: Early descent into the Tumpak Sewu waterfall canyon (approximately 120 m curtain waterfall, canyon wade). Transfer north to the Bromo sector. Mount Bromo sunrise and crater visit by private 4WD jeep. Return to Surabaya. Tour concludes in Surabaya.

subjectOf.name: Kawah Ijen
subjectOf.description: Active stratovolcano with the world's largest acidic crater lake and the world's largest visible blue fire phenomenon. Elevation 2,386 m.

subjectOf.name: Papuma Beach
subjectOf.description: Scenic south-coast beach in Jember with white sand, limestone cliff formations, and clear Indian Ocean waters. A rarely-visited coastal stop on the Ijen circuit.

subjectOf.name: Tumpak Sewu Waterfall
subjectOf.description: Indonesia's most dramatic curtain waterfall, approximately 120 m high. Reached via a technical canyon descent through narrow gorge passages and river crossings in Lumajang.

subjectOf.name: Mount Bromo
subjectOf.description: Active volcano in the Tengger Semeru National Park. Penanjakan sunrise viewpoint and crater base accessed by private 4WD jeep. Elevation 2,329 m.

offers.description: Per-person: IDR 8,050,000 (solo) · IDR 4,700,000 (2) · IDR 4,350,000 (3) · IDR 4,000,000 (4–5) · IDR 3,600,000 (6–7) · IDR 3,275,000 (8–10) · IDR 3,125,000 (11+). All-inclusive: private transport, crew, 3 nights accommodation + breakfast, entrance fees, 4WD jeep (Bromo), gas masks + trekking poles (Ijen), health screening, mineral water, T-shirt.

mainEntity.name: What is included in the 4 Day Ijen, Papuma Beach, Tumpak Sewu & Bromo tour price?

acceptedAnswer.text: All JVTO packages include: private transport (AC MPV for 1–3 guests, Toyota Hiace for 4–9), dedicated crew, all entrance fees and permits, mineral water daily, full pick-up to drop-off assistance, and a JVTO travel T-shirt. This tour additionally includes 3 nights accommodation + breakfast, a private 4WD jeep for Mount Bromo, gas masks and trekking poles for Ijen, and health-screening coordination as required by BBKSDA regulations. International flights, travel insurance, and personal expenses are not included.

mainEntity.name: How does the Ijen health screening work?

acceptedAnswer.text: For tours that include the Ijen night hike, JVTO arranges health-screening coordination — conducted by licensed medical staff — as required under BBKSDA access regulations for the Ijen crater zone (Surat Edaran SE.1658/KSA.9/2024). Vitals measured digitally include SpO2, blood pressure, heart rate, and respiratory history. The certificate carries a QR code verified by BBKSDA at the crater access gate. No valid QR code means no access to the crater zone. Screening is coordinated the evening of Day 1 in Bondowoso.

mainEntity.name: What happens if I do not pass the Ijen health screening?

acceptedAnswer.text: If screening staff or the JVTO team advise that you should not attempt the hike, JVTO follows that decision — it protects your safety and others on the trail. The climb is cancelled for that individual (not the group); alternatives include waiting at base camp or a safe zone. The screening session cost is non-refundable. The group continues.

mainEntity.name: What happens if Bromo or Ijen is closed on my tour dates?

acceptedAnswer.text: JVTO provides a written Plan B briefed before departure, not improvised at the gate. If a site closes due to volcanic activity or park authority order, an alternative route is offered. Guests may rebook at no penalty if the primary sites are inaccessible on the booked dates due to official closure. JVTO monitors MAGMA Indonesia / PVMBG and BBKSDA advisories daily and contacts guests proactively if closures are imminent.

mainEntity.name: Is this a private tour or a shared group tour?

acceptedAnswer.text: 100% private. Your booking covers your own vehicle, your own driver-guide (or driver + escort guide for larger groups), and your own itinerary. No strangers are added to your tour at any point. JVTO does not operate shared group tours.
